finding.summary: Severe intersample overshoots: true peak (-5.5 dBTP) is 10.5 dB higher than sample peak (-16.0 dBFS). This indicates a waveform with high intersample peaks, risking distortion on DA conversion or after nonlinear processing.
finding.probable_cause: The synth generates a waveform with sharp transitions or internal clipping, leading to large intersample peaks that are not captured by sample-peak metering.
finding.confidence_reason: Verified isolated capture but the 10.5 dB gap between true and sample peaks is unusually large and warrants cautious gain staging.

evidence_refs.description: True peak level measured at -5.5 dBTP

proposal.reason: Reduce intersample peak level to prevent potential distortion in subsequent processing or DA conversion. Lowering the track volume by 6 dB brings the true peak to -11.5 dBTP, a safer margin.
